candidates.comparison: compiled with async TP fused gemm comms pass (VLLM_COMPILE + fuse_gemm_comms=True) vs standard TP without compilation
candidates.oracle: exact dict equality via compare_two_settings -> compare_all_settings -> assert ref_result == compare_result (line 1342 of tests/utils.py)
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: agree — compare_two_settings calls _test_completion which collects text, finish_reason, usage into dicts; compare_all_settings asserts exact dict equality at line 1342. This is exact text/token equality, a weak oracle.
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: agree — async_tp_args includes compilation_config with VLLM_COMPILE mode and fuse_gemm_comms=True, while tp_args has no compilation config. Fusing gemm with communications changes accumulation order, which can realistically alter floating-point results and produce different generated text.
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: agree — Not Strong #2: non-compiled vs compiled mode parity hidden inside compare_two_settings. Not Strong #5: async TP fused distributed compile-pass parity against unfused baseline. No strong contract guarantees compiled with fused gemm comms produces identical text to uncompiled TP.
candidates.code_snippet:

    model_id,
    async_tp_args,
    tp_args,
    method="generate",
    force_v1_runner=True,
)

candidates.comparison: compiled with sequence parallel pass (VLLM_COMPILE + enable_sp=True, eliminate_noops=True) vs standard TP without compilation
candidates.oracle: exact dict equality via compare_two_settings -> compare_all_settings -> assert ref_result == compare_result (line 1342 of tests/utils.py)
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: agree — _compare_sp calls compare_two_settings with method='generate', which collects text/finish_reason/usage dicts and asserts exact equality. Weak oracle confirmed.
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: agree — sequence parallel replaces all-reduce with reduce-scatter + all-gather, fundamentally changing the accumulation order of partial results across GPUs. The tp_sp_args side has compilation with SP pass; the tp_args side has no compilation. FP drift from reordered reductions can realistically change generated text.
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: agree — Not Strong #5: sequence parallel compile-pass parity against unfused baseline. Not Strong #2: non-compiled vs compiled mode parity inside compare_two_settings. The SP pass changes the communication pattern, and no contract guarantees text-identical output.
